programme.desc: Oliver Wheeler's great-grandson Jeff Wheeler and explorer and author Wade Davis travel in Oliver's footsteps, retracing that first expedition and rediscovering adventures and achievements.

programme.title: Still Standing
programme.desc: For 50 years, this tiny town was an air force base where thousands of pilots learned to fly; when the base closed, Southport transformed into a new kind of community.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: There's still so much not known about fear; a look at whether fear is good for people and what life would be like without it.

programme.title: The Killer Within
programme.desc: Looking at DNA evidence that allowed detectives to nab a killer many years later.

programme.title: Killer Cases

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at the development of situation comedies, tracing their evolution from the 1950s to the 21st century, beginning by looking at the depictions of families.

programme.title: Laurel and Hardy: Their Lives and Magic
programme.desc: Documentary shedding new light on the turbulent private lives of Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy and the genuine friendship between them.

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva.

programme.desc: The former NFL player talks about his idol.

programme.desc: Documentary about elusive Canadian climber Marc-André Leclerc, who has made some of the boldest solo ascents in history, away from cameras and with no margin for error.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: I Am Johnny Cash
programme.desc: A celebration of the country singer-songwriter built around 12 of his classic tracks spanning four decades, and featuring interviews with his family and friends. Featuring contributions from Sheryl Crow, Kris Kristofferson, Willie Nelson, John Mellencamp and Kid Rock.

programme.title: Val
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the daily life of actor Val Kilmer, featuring never-before-seen footage spanning 40 years.

programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, who rose to fame through his roles in Romeo + Juliet and Titanic, and won a Best Actor Oscar for The Revenant.

programme.title: The Eighties
programme.desc: A look at how a mysterious and lethal illness known as Aids developed into a pandemic with enormous political and cultural consequences.

programme.desc: The journey through the history of cinema concludes with a look at films including the Harry Potter and Lord of the Rings series.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: How the American Civil War became a crusade to end slavery. The year 1862 saw political fighting threaten President Lincoln's administration and Union general George McClellan's ill-fated campaign on the Virginia Peninsula, where his huge army met a smaller but more resourceful Confederate force. Eventually, rumours began to circulate that Europe was prepared to recognise the Confederacy.

programme.desc: A look at comedy shows set in the workplace, which offer insights into the state of the American workforce.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset welcomes its first saki monkey and the park's vets struggle to fathom what is bothering ring-tailed lemur Houdini.

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple who flips homes uses all their experience to buy and renovate an outdated beach house straight out of the 1970s; they put in plenty of sweat equity to keep the costs low and get the home ready for the next family of buyers.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: First-time homeowners from the Jersey shore are looking to escape the cold and find their piece of paradise in Madeira Beach, Fla.; they're hoping to find a home with water views that they can renovate as a place to raise their family.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Willing to put in the renovation work themselves, a young couple expecting their first child searches for an outdated house to transform into a forever home--for a fraction of the price of new waterfront properties in Bristol, Rhode Island.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The guys travel to West Alexander, Pa., the heart of farm country, to save a classic bank barn; Johnny visits a local farmer and her pasture-raised animals; Mark drops by a local farmers' market to buy goodies for the crew.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark Bowe and his designer Karen Tillery take steps to realize his dream of building a small village of finished antique structures in White Sulphur Springs, West Virginia.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders travel to save a fine example of a Pennsylvania bank barn; crafted from white oak, the timber frame features a style of diagonal bracing they've never encountered before.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Nashville couple wants a waterfront retreat on Alabama's Smith Lake to escape their hectic lives; they may have to take on major renovations to create space where they can make memories with their kids and entertain friends and family.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Mark and Mary are looking for a lake house that can serve as a meeting place for their large family; they customize an Ohio lakefront home for their large family.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Minnesota is known as the Land of 10,000 Lakes and there's no better place to find the perfect house on the perfect lake; with lakefront prices at an all-time high, this couple will need to create their dream retreat with some hands-on renovations.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: An Atlanta couple fight budget restraints as they seek the perfect vacation home for their blended family in Jackson Lake, Ga.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: In New Sharon, Maine, Chase and his team help Rod save his rundown camp from falling into the pristine Kimball Pond; he'd like to patch up the holes and get his wife to come back to the camp, which means it's going to need a lot of work.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: When their business manager requests a brand-new build over a vacation-packed winter, the team loses track of the budget as time slips away and snow piles up; the crew try to finish the build without going any more over budget.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: After a tree falls onto the roof of a family camp, the mishap is chosen as an opportunity; the "Maine Cabin Masters" are called in to facilitate an expansion.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team restore the old army barracks, which have provided overflow sleeping accommodations for more than 40 years at the Morrill family camp; the guys strive to save the structure for their growing family and future family reunions.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Harringtons purchased a tranquil plot of oceanside property in Phippsburg way back in the 1950s; Joe Harrington, a Maine Basketball Hall of Famer, cleared a parcel so he can construct a camp of his own to add to the family's legacy.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The team tackles a historic landmark restoration, complete with rotted siding and spooky baby dolls.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A lake-loving family outgrows their legacy cabin; the gang tackles the challenges of remodeling the mazelike layout as Ashley uses an heirloom for a special project, all in time for the family's annual 4th of July party.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Spruce Mountain Ski Lodge is in need of vital repairs in order to keep serving the community; the guys demo with a whole new twist, Ashley finds a scaly friend to introduce to Ryan and Chase lands the season's first jump.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The crew acquires a retired lumberyard and builds out a new wood shop for projects; they also revamp their headquarters to improve flow.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The crew helps a couple turn their chopped up, suburban cabin into the spacious, rustic Maine getaway they've always wanted; with the help of some new faces and upgraded equipment, the Cabin Masters breathe new life into this family's retreat.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family buys a property with everything except a safe structure to call home; the team swoop in to save the day, combating a nor'easter along the way, to create a four season haven.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: After a massive storm in central Maine, the Cabin Masters return to two former renovations to help out. Multiple trees destroyed cabins and porches around the YMCA camp property while the Chadwick family's screened-in porch was crushed by a tree.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The "Maine Cabin Masters" team up with the Center for Entrepreneurial Studies, a local nonprofit that develops business opportunities for disadvantaged youth, to create a small house for its participants.
